plays.title: The Three Miners in the Kuttenberg
plays.description: Three pious miners are trapped in a mine for seven years until their final wishes are granted.

plays.title: The Mountain Spirit
plays.description: The mountain spirit Meister Hämmerling appears in various forms in the silver mines to punish or reward miners.

plays.title: The Mountain Monk in the Harz
plays.description: Two miners are saved from darkness and helped by a giant monk, until their drunken boast makes the magic oil vanish.

plays.title: Mother Holle's Pond
plays.description: The pond on Hoher Meißner acts as the gateway to Mother Holle's underworld castle where she rewards the industrious.

plays.title: Mother Holle Wanders
plays.description: During the twelve nights of Christmas, Mother Holle wanders to reward industrious spinners and punish the lazy.

plays.title: Mother Holle's Bath
plays.description: At noon, Mother Holle bathes in her sacred pool on Hoher Meißner, demanding respect from all who pass by.

plays.title: Frau Holla and Faithful Eckart
plays.description: The legend of Frau Holla passing through Schwarza accompanied by the faithful Eckart who warns travelers.

plays.title: Frau Holla and the Peasant
plays.description: Frau Holla rewards a helpful peasant who repairs her carriage wheel with wood shavings that turn into pure gold.

plays.title: The Springing Root
plays.description: A shepherd uses a magical spring-root to open the locked doors inside the Köterberg and gather treasures.

plays.title: The Maidens of Boyneburg
plays.description: Three noble maidens on the Boyneburg await a predicted death by lightning, showing submission to God's will.

plays.title: The Pielberg
plays.description: A beautiful banished virgin appears at noon on the Pielberg, warning people to stay away.

plays.title: The Castle Maiden
plays.description: A maiden carrying a large keyring appears at noon on the Schlossberg, bathing in the fountain before returning.

plays.title: The Snake Maiden
plays.description: Leonhard enters a subterranean vault where a half-snake maiden promises treasures, but he flees in terror.

plays.title: The Heavy Child
plays.description: Two noblemen find a child in a bush that cannot be lifted, which prophesies a fruitful but deadly year.

plays.title: The Old Wine Cellar near Salurn
plays.description: Christoph finds a wine cellar guarded by three old men, but after revealing the secret, he is killed.

plays.title: Giants' Game
plays.description: Giants playing on the Brunsberg throw a ball into the valley, forming the Knäuelwiese before their demise.

plays.title: The Giants' Toy
plays.description: A giant's daughter takes a ploughing peasant as a toy, but her father teaches her to respect the humans who feed them.

plays.title: Giant Einheer
plays.description: The giant warrior Einheer wades through rivers, pulls his horse by the tail, and fights like an entire army.

plays.title: Giants' Pillars
plays.description: Nine stone pillars in the forest near Miltenberg bear the handprints of giants who tried to build a bridge.

plays.title: The Köterberg
plays.description: A shepherd finds the entrance to gold inside the Köterberg but is chased away by a bloody man.

plays.title: Geroldseck
plays.description: Ancient German heroes rest inside Castle Geroldseck, waiting to emerge and save their people in times of need.

plays.title: Kaiser Karl at Nuremberg
plays.description: Kaiser Karl sits in the deep well of Nuremberg Castle, his beard grown through a stone table.

plays.title: Barbarossa in the Kyffhaeuser
plays.description: Emperor Frederick Barbarossa sleeps inside the Kyffhäuser, awaiting the day the ravens stop flying to return.

plays.title: The Pear Tree on Walserfeld
plays.description: A dry pear tree on the Walserfeld predicts a terrible final battle when it greens and bears fruit.

plays.title: The Enchanted King at Schildheiss
plays.description: Workers find an enchanted king and a maiden in Castle Schildheiß who turns into a fire-breathing snake.

plays.title: Kaiser Karl's Departure
plays.description: Before a war, the Odenberg opens and Kaiser Karl emerges with his army, sounding his horn.

plays.title: The Untersberg
plays.description: The Untersberg near Salzburg contains palaces, churches, and treasures guarded by small midnight-walking dwarfs.

plays.title: Kaiser Karl in the Untersberg
plays.description: Kaiser Karl sits in the Untersberg with a golden crown, his beard growing around the table until the end of the world.

plays.title: Scherfenberger and the Dwarf
plays.description: Knight Wilhelm von Scherfenberg receives a protective ring from a dwarf king, but dies on the battlefield.

plays.title: The Silent People at Plesse
plays.description: Helpful dwarfs called the silent people live in Castle Plesse, punishing human offenses through their cattle.

plays.title: The Little People's Wedding
plays.description: Dwarfs slip into Eilenburg Castle at night to celebrate a wedding, inviting the old Count to join.

plays.title: Dwarfs Turned to Stone
plays.description: In a valley near Elnbogen, helpful dwarfs are turned to stone by their angry ruler when they go to a wedding.

plays.title: Dwarf Mountains
plays.description: Near Aachen, Jena, and Hohenstein, dwarfs borrow kitchen utensils from humans for their weddings and return them.

plays.title: Dwarfs Borrow Bread
plays.description: A dwarf woman borrows fresh bread from a farmer, returning a warm loaf, but warns that human sins drive them away.

plays.title: The Count of Hoia
plays.description: A dwarf asks the Count of Hoia to host a nightly feast in his castle, rewarding him with a sword and a ring.

plays.title: Dwarfs Driven Out
plays.description: The noise of hammers and stamp mills in the Ore Mountains drives the dwarfs away, though they promise to return.

plays.title: The Wichts
plays.description: Tiny dwarfs dressed as miners haunt the pits, teasing workers and knocking three times as an omen of death.

plays.title: Conjuring the Dwarfs
plays.description: Paul Creuz conjures dwarfs using milk, honey, and a black hen, eventually calling their King for a book of wisdom.

plays.title: The Dwarf at the Dance
plays.description: A dwarf from the Wunderberg joins a wedding in Glaß, dances with the maidens, and demands honorable behavior.

plays.title: The Cellar Dwarf
plays.description: A cellar dwarf in Lützen prays alongside a maid; later, a fire destroys the town but spares his house.

plays.title: The Ancestress of Rantzau
plays.description: A dwarf leads the grandmother of Rantzau to help a Zwergin give birth, rewarding her with gold under strict warnings.

plays.title: Hermann of Rosenberg
plays.description: After Hermann von Rosenberg's feast, earth spirits request to hold their own wedding in his hall, promising peace.

plays.title: The Osenberg Dwarfs
plays.description: Osenberg dwarfs pay for beer with silver; a forgotten jug brings luck to the family until it breaks.

plays.title: The Earth Spirit and the Shepherd Boy
plays.description: A shepherd boy frees a dwarf trapped in a stone; the dwarf moves into a neighbor's house, refusing to leave.

plays.title: The Lodging Dwarf
plays.description: A wet dwarf seeks shelter in a storm; only a poor pious couple takes him in, saving them from a devastating landslide.

plays.title: Zeitelmoos
plays.description: A rider meets two children laughing in the Zeitelmoos forest; they reappear ahead of him, laughing mockingly.

plays.title: The Moss Woman
plays.description: A woodcutter Hans Krepel refuses a moss woman's plea to carve crosses in the last tree stump, triggering her wrath.

plays.title: The Wild Hunt and the Moss People
plays.description: The Moss People dress in green moss to hide from the Wild Hunt that storms through the forests of Saalfeld.

plays.title: The Water Sprite
plays.description: A midwife is summoned to help the Water Sprite's wife; she is warned to accept only modest pay or face death.

plays.title: The Wild Women of the Untersberg
plays.description: Wild women emerge from the Untersberg to feed shepherd children and assist with the harvest before returning.

plays.title: Dance with the Water Sprite
plays.description: A water sprite with an icy hand dances with Ursula at a festival in Laibach, eventually dragging her into the river.

plays.title: The Water Sprite and the Farmer
plays.description: A friendly water sprite invites his neighboring farmer to visit his home beneath the lake.

plays.title: The Water Sprite at the Butcher's Bench
plays.description: A butcher cuts the finger of a water sprite who always pays for meat with holed coins, causing him to vanish.

plays.title: The Swimmer
plays.description: A boastful baker's apprentice bets he can swim across the Elbe three times, only to be dragged down by a Nix.

plays.title: Brother Nickel
plays.description: Fishermen on Rügen find their boat placed high in a tree by a hidden water spirit named Brother Nickel.

plays.title: The Nix Well
plays.description: Nixes appear at the Nix Well near Kirchhain, and another nix is sighted at the Elisabeth mill in Marburg.

plays.title: The Magdeburg Nixes
plays.description: A nix drags a skilled swimmer into the depths of the Elbe and later thwarts the construction of a water pipe.

plays.title: The Dönges Lake
plays.description: Two nixes flee a village festival after a youth steals their gloves, causing the lake to turn blood-red.

plays.title: Mummelsee
plays.description: Throwing stones into the bottomless Mummelsee triggers severe storms until water sprites return the stones.

plays.title: The Elbe Maiden and the Saale Woman
plays.description: Nixes disguised as clean town girls shop at meat markets, recognizable only by a wet corner of their aprons.

plays.title: Water Right
plays.description: A river claims its annual human sacrifice at Leipzig, while nixes dance on the water before a drowning.

plays.title: The Drowned Child
plays.description: A grieving mother collects her drowned child's bones from the lake; when placed on the altar, the child revives.

plays.title: Slit-Ears
plays.description: A malicious water spirit named Slit-Ears lurks in the river Streu to drag crossing travelers to their deaths.

plays.title: The Water Nix and the Miller's Apprentice
plays.description: An apprentice shoots at a nix combing her hair on the river; three days later, he drowns while bathing.

plays.title: Before the Nixes Oregano and Horehound Help
plays.description: A midwife helping a nix-wife give birth under the Saale river uses protective herbs to escape the nix's power.

plays.title: The Nix's Legs
plays.description: A barber shines a light on a nix's legs at midnight, exposing the water spirit and forcing it to vanish.

plays.title: The Maid and the Nix
plays.description: A maid serves a nix under the river for three years, leaving because the food is always completely unsalted.

plays.title: The Lady of Alvensleben
plays.description: A noblewoman assists a nix-wife giving birth inside a mountain, receiving a golden ring that seals her lineage's fate.

plays.title: The Lady of Hahn and the Nix
plays.description: A noblewoman helping a nix-wife under the river refuses ashes, earning three gold pieces that sustain her house.

plays.title: The Lady of Bonikau
plays.description: A lady in childbed allows earthmen to celebrate a wedding in her room, hosting an invisible festive assembly.

plays.title: The Measuring Laddle, the Ring and the Cup
plays.description: A dying count divides his lands and leaves three magical fairy gifts to his daughters to secure their fortunes.

plays.title: The Peasant and His Kobold
plays.description: A peasant tries to burn down his barn to escape a kobold, only to find the spirit riding along on his cart.

plays.title: The Kobold in the Mill
plays.description: Two students shelter in a mill where one eats the food set out for the house spirit, earning a midnight thrashing.

plays.title: Little Hat
plays.description: A house spirit wearing a small felt hat serves the bishop of Hildesheim, but takes bloody revenge when mocked.

plays.title: Hinzelmann
plays.description: The house spirit Hinzelmann haunts Castle Hudemühlen, keeping house, speaking to guests, and giving warnings.

plays.title: Knocker
plays.description: A helpful household spirit in Flügelau Castle flees in a trail of fire when forced to reveal his true form.

plays.title: The Frog King
plays.description: The youngest princess loses her dearest plaything, the golden ball, down a deep well.

plays.title: Cat and Mouse in Partnership
plays.description: A cat and a mouse set up house together as partners.

plays.title: Our Lady's Child
plays.description: The Virgin Mary takes in a woodcutter's starving child and later punishes her secret disobedience.

plays.title: The Story of the Youth Who Went Forth to Learn What Fear Was
plays.description: A fearless boy sets out into the world to learn what shuddering and fear even mean.

plays.title: The Wolf and the Seven Young Kids
plays.description: A wicked wolf devours seven kids, but the mother saves them and drowns the wolf in the well.

plays.title: Faithful John
plays.description: A dying king entrusts faithful Johannes with the care of his young son.

plays.title: The Nightingale and the Blindworm
plays.description: Two one-eyed creatures share a house until a borrowed eye breeds betrayal and lasting enmity.

plays.title: The Good Bargain
plays.description: A poor farmer drives his cow to market and sells her for a seemingly worthless promise.

plays.title: The Strange Musician
plays.description: A lonely fiddler wanders the forest, luring the animals with his violin.

plays.title: The Hand with the Knife
plays.description: A slighted girl gets a magic knife from an elf, until her brothers hack off the helper's arm.

plays.title: The Twelve Brothers
plays.description: A king resolves to kill his twelve sons if a daughter is born.

plays.title: The Pack of Ragamuffins
plays.description: A proud cock and hen go travelling and cheat their innkeeper.

plays.title: Brother and Sister
plays.description: A little brother and sister flee their wicked stepmother.

plays.title: Rapunzel
plays.description: A man steals rampion for his pregnant wife from a dreaded sorceress's garden.

plays.title: The Three Little Men in the Wood
plays.description: An envious stepmother drives her lovely stepdaughter into the winter woods in a paper dress.

plays.title: The Three Spinners
plays.description: Three strange spinners help a lazy girl master the queen's impossible spinning task.

plays.title: Hansel and Gretel
plays.description: Two children abandoned in the forest outwit a wicked witch and return home rich to their father.

plays.title: The Three Snake-Leaves
plays.description: A poor youth goes to war, wins through bravery, and gains the princess as his wife.

plays.title: Mr Fix and Ready
plays.description: A cunning servant wins his king the princess with grateful animals and rises to first minister.

plays.title: The White Snake
plays.description: A curious servant secretly tastes the king's covered dish, a white snake.

plays.title: Straw, Coal, and Bean
plays.description: In a kitchen a straw, a coal, and a bean escape death by fire.

plays.title: The Fisherman and His Wife
plays.description: A poor fisherman catches a talking flounder that reveals itself as an enchanted prince.

plays.title: The Brave Little Tailor
plays.description: A little tailor swats seven flies on his jam bread and sets out into the world.

plays.title: Cinderella
plays.description: A pious, hardworking girl loses her own mother and is tormented by her stepmother.

plays.title: The Riddle
plays.description: A prince and his faithful servant set out into the world and lose their way in a dark forest.

plays.title: How Children Played at Slaughtering
plays.description: Children playing at slaughtering a sow set off an inquiry with grave consequences.

plays.title: The Mouse, the Bird, and the Sausage
plays.description: A mouse, a bird, and a sausage live together in a happy little household.

plays.title: Mother Holle
plays.description: A widow favours her lazy own daughter and forces her hardworking stepdaughter to toil.

plays.title: The Seven Ravens
plays.description: An angry father curses his seven sons into ravens, and their sister sets out to find them.

plays.title: Little Red Cap
plays.description: A wolf tricks Little Red Cap and swallows her and her grandmother, until a huntsman saves them.

plays.title: The Town Musicians of Bremen
plays.description: Four old animals flee death and scare robbers from a forest house with one loud concert.

plays.title: Death and the Goose Boy
plays.description: A contented goose-boy follows Death to the next world while a miser is punished for his greed.

plays.title: The Singing Bone
plays.description: A man kills his brother for the boar-hunt's glory and is exposed years later by a singing bone.

plays.title: The Devil with the Three Golden Hairs
plays.description: A child of fortune escapes a king's plots and fetches three golden hairs from the Devil himself.

plays.title: The Louse and the Flea
plays.description: A small brewing mishap plunges house and nature into grief and ends in a flood.

plays.title: The Girl Without Hands
plays.description: A maimed girl flees the Devil and is saved and healed by God's grace and a faithful king.

plays.title: Clever Hans
plays.description: Hans takes his mother's advice literally and ruins his own courtship of his bride Gretel.

plays.title: The Three Languages
plays.description: A cast-out son learns the animals' speech, finds a treasure, and rises by providence to pope.

plays.title: Puss in Boots
plays.description: A crafty cat wins his poor master wealth, a castle, and a princess with boots, a sack, and guile.

plays.title: Clever Elsie
plays.description: A supposedly clever bride loses herself in catastrophic worries and forgets who she even is.

plays.title: Hans's Trine
plays.description: Lazy Trine, doubting who she is over a shortened skirt, leaves home never to return.

plays.title: The Tailor in Heaven
plays.description: A tailor sneaks into Heaven, sits on God's throne, and is banished for his self-righteousness.

plays.title: The Wishing-Table
plays.description: Three brothers gain magic gifts, are robbed by an innkeeper, and win them back with a cudgel.

plays.title: Thumbling
plays.description: A tiny boy helps his father, escapes kidnappers and thieves, and is freed from a wolf's belly.

plays.title: The Table Napkin, the Knapsack, the Cannon Hat, and the Horn
plays.description: Three sons gain magic devices, rout enemy armies, and rescue their needy mother.

plays.title: The Wedding of Mrs. Fox
plays.description: Mr Fox feigns death to test his wife, who turns away every suitor but a nine-tailed one.

plays.title: The Elves
plays.description: Two naked elves secretly make shoes for a poor cobbler until he gifts them clothes.

plays.title: The Robber Bridegroom
plays.description: A miller's daughter visits her sinister groom's forest house and unmasks him as a murderer.

plays.title: Herr Korbes
plays.description: A cock and hen travel with an odd company to wicked Herr Korbes's house and punish him.

plays.title: The Godfather
plays.description: A poor father seeks a godfather, grows famous through a magic water, and meets the Devil.

plays.title: Mother Trudy
plays.description: A girl ignores her parents' warning, visits the eerie Frau Trude, and is burned by her.

plays.title: The Strange Feast
plays.description: A blood sausage lures the liver sausage into a deadly trap she just escapes through a warning.

plays.title: Godfather Death
plays.description: Death makes a poor boy a famous doctor, then punishes his betrayal with his own life-candle.

plays.title: Thumbling's Travels
plays.description: The thumb-sized tailor's son roams the world, aids robbers, outwits guards, and escapes a wolf.

plays.title: Fitcher's Bird
plays.description: A sorcerer abducts sisters but is outwitted by the cleverest, who escapes disguised as a bird.

plays.title: The Juniper Tree
plays.description: A wicked stepmother kills her little stepson, who rises as a fair bird and takes just revenge.

plays.title: Old Sultan
plays.description: An old dog, marked for death as useless, makes a pact with the wolf and proves his loyalty.

plays.title: The Six Swans
plays.description: A sister sews six shirts in silence to free her brothers from their swan enchantment.

plays.title: Briar Rose
plays.description: A princess falls into a hundred-year sleep from a spindle, until a prince wakes her.

plays.title: Foundling-Bird
plays.description: Two foster children flee a wicked cook by transforming again and again as they run.

plays.title: King Thrushbeard
plays.description: A haughty princess mocks every suitor, is married off to a minstrel as punishment, and humbled.

plays.title: Little Snow-White
plays.description: A fair princess flees her wicked stepmother to seven dwarfs and is rescued.

plays.title: The Knapsack, the Hat, and the Horn
plays.description: A poor brother trades a wishing-cloth for magic weapons, beats the king, and crushes the betrayal.

plays.title: Foolish Hans
plays.description: A lad thought simple is chosen as father by a lemon, wishes his fortune up, and becomes king.

plays.title: Rumpelstiltskin
plays.description: A miller's daughter must spin straw into gold, promises her child, and saves it by guessing his name.

plays.title: Sweetheart Roland
plays.description: A girl flees the witch with Roland, survives his forgetting, and wins him back through her singing.

plays.title: The Golden Bird
plays.description: A prince seeks the golden bird, wins horse and bride with a fox's help, and redeems his helper.

plays.title: The Dog and the Sparrow
plays.description: A dog runs off and allies with a faithful sparrow, who avenges his death on a cruel carter.

plays.title: Frederick and Catherine
plays.description: A couple suffers absurd mishaps through the wife's folly before scaring thieves from a tree.

plays.title: Prince Swan
plays.description: A girl follows an enchanted swan, wins golden gifts of heaven, and frees her love on the glass mountain.

plays.title: The Two Brothers
plays.description: Twins gain wealth from a gold bird, brave dragons and witches, and keep faith with each other.

plays.title: The Golden Egg
plays.description: Poor broom-maker's children find a bird whose golden heart and liver raise them to crown and wealth.

plays.title: The Little Peasant
plays.description: A poor peasant outwits rich neighbours, prospers through lies, and lures them to their ruin.

plays.title: The Tailor Who Became Rich Quickly
plays.description: A cunning tailor grows hugely rich through fraud and brings the whole village under his thumb.

plays.title: The Queen Bee
plays.description: Simpleton shields weak animals from his cruel brothers and, with their help, frees a petrified castle.

plays.title: Bluebeard
plays.description: A young wife breaks her husband Bluebeard's ban and is saved by her brothers at the last moment.

plays.title: The Three Feathers
plays.description: A king blows three feathers to the wind; Simpleton wins the throne through an underground toad's gifts.

plays.title: The Golden Goose
plays.description: A king offers his daughter to whoever makes her laugh; Simpleton does it with a golden goose.

plays.title: The White Dove
plays.description: An underrated prince follows a fruit-stealing dove and frees a little man and a princess.

plays.title: Allerleirauh
plays.description: A princess flees disguised in a fur cloak and is known to the king by three golden trinkets.

plays.title: The Hare's Bride
plays.description: A girl follows a hare to his hut and escapes the forced wedding by leaving a straw decoy.

plays.title: Hurleburlebutz
plays.description: A lost king promises his youngest daughter to a wood-imp who exposes the true bride by a test.

plays.title: The Twelve Huntsmen
plays.description: A jilted bride and eleven companions disguise as huntsmen and win the king back by guile.

plays.title: The Thief and His Master
plays.description: A father learns thieving for his son, who beats the master in a duel of magical transformations.

plays.title: The Summer and Winter Garden
plays.description: A merchant promises his youngest daughter to a beast for a rose from a summer-and-winter garden.

plays.title: Jorinda and Joringel
plays.description: Two lovers fall under a sorceress's spell until Joringel frees his bride with a magic flower.

plays.title: The Three Sons of Fortune
plays.description: A father leaves his three sons a cock, a scythe, and a cat, with which they make their fortunes.

plays.title: The Okerlo
plays.description: A princess raised on an ogre's island flees with a prince through magical transformations.

plays.title: How Six Travelled through the World
plays.description: A discharged soldier and five gifted companions set out to wrest the king's unjust fortune.

plays.title: Princess Mouseskin
plays.description: A cast-out princess serves unknown in a mouseskin gown until a ring reveals her at table.

plays.title: The Wolf and the Man
plays.description: A boastful wolf picks a fight with a man and is painfully taught better by a huntsman.

plays.title: The Pear Won't Fall
plays.description: A master tries in vain to shake a pear down through disobedient helpers, until the knacker is called.

plays.title: The Wolf and the Fox
plays.description: A greedy wolf forces a fox to serve him until the fox lures him into a trap in the farmer's cellar.

plays.title: The Murder Castle
plays.description: A cobbler's daughter follows a rich lord to his castle, uncovers his murders, and escapes in a hay cart.

plays.title: The Fox and His Cousin
plays.description: A she-wolf makes the fox her gossip; he tempts her to steal sheep and lets her carry him home.

plays.title: Johannes-Wassersprung and Caspar-Wassersprung
plays.description: Two water-born brothers set out with animals, slay a dragon, and free each other from a witch.

plays.title: The Fox and the Cat
plays.description: A haughty fox boasts to a cat of his hundred tricks but fails hopelessly against the hounds.

plays.title: The Phoenix
plays.description: A youth found by the river escapes a steward's plots and fetches three feathers from the phoenix.

plays.title: The Pink
plays.description: A prince with the gift of wishing exposes a false cook, saves his mother, and wins the maiden.

plays.title: Clever Gretel
plays.description: A sly cook devours the feast and, with a quick lie, sets the guest and her master against each other.

plays.title: The Carpenter and Turner
plays.description: A prince abducts a princess with artificial wings, escapes the stake, and claims his kingdom.

plays.title: The Old Man and His Grandson
plays.description: A cast-out grandfather returns to the table when the child shames the parents' pride.

plays.title: The Water-Nixie
plays.description: Two children fall down a well, are enslaved by the water-nixie, and escape by throwing magic objects.

plays.title: The Death of the Little Hen
plays.description: The greedy hen chokes on a nut, and grief for her drags everyone into death.

plays.title: Brother Lustig
plays.description: A merry soldier travels with Saint Peter, secretly eats the lamb's heart, and wishes himself to Heaven.

plays.title: The Smith and the Devil
plays.description: A smith makes a pact with the Devil, outwits him with a sack, and nails imps to Hell's gate.

plays.title: Gambling Hansel
plays.description: An incorrigible gambler stakes away even the Lord's gifts and is scattered at Heaven's gate.

plays.title: The Three Sisters
plays.description: A prince frees his three sisters given to beast-bridegrooms and breaks their enchantment.

plays.title: Hans in Luck
plays.description: Hans cheerfully trades away one good thing after another until he returns home free and happy.

plays.title: Hans Married
plays.description: A cunning uncle swindles poor Hans a rich bride with a patched trouser and a single coin.

plays.title: The Mother-in-Law
plays.description: A wicked mother-in-law locks her daughter-in-law and grandchildren in the cellar to devour them.

plays.title: The Gold-Children
plays.description: From a divided gold fish grow two gold children whose lilies decide over life and death.

plays.title: Snow Flower
plays.description: A winter-born princess seeks healing herbs for her sick mother and is made lovely by bees.

plays.title: The Princess with the Louse
plays.description: A tidy princess fattens a louse calf-big, makes a gown from its hide, and riddles her suitors.

plays.title: Of Prince Johannes
plays.description: Prince Johannes flies with an apparition to the red castle and, after trials, beholds the sun-princess.

plays.title: The Good Cloth
plays.description: Two sisters own a cloth that turns all to gold, until the foolish one swaps it for a new rag.

plays.title: The Fox and the Geese
plays.description: A fox means to eat a flock of geese, but their endless prayer saves them.

plays.title: The Poor Man and the Rich Man
plays.description: The Lord rewards the poor man's welcome and punishes the rich man's greed with his own wishes.

plays.title: The Singing, Springing Lark
plays.description: A faithful daughter follows her enchanted husband to the end of the world.

plays.title: The Goose-Girl
plays.description: A true bride, ousted by her maid, herds geese as a goose-girl and is recognised at last.

plays.title: The Young Giant
plays.description: A thumb-sized peasant's son grows into a giant's mighty man and pays every spite with his fist.

plays.title: The Gnome
plays.description: Foolish Hans conquers the gnome and the dragons, is betrayed, and in the end wins the princess anyway.

plays.title: The King of the Golden Mountain
plays.description: A son sold away becomes king, is betrayed, and wins his realm back with three tricks.

plays.title: The Raven
plays.description: An enchanted princess waits as a raven for redemption by a faithful man.

plays.title: The Peasant's Wise Daughter
plays.description: A clever peasant's daughter solves the king's riddles and, in the end, wins him back by wit.

plays.title: Old Hildebrand
plays.description: A parson sends the farmer on a made-up pilgrimage, but the gossip brings him home and exposes it.

plays.title: The Three Little Birds
plays.description: An imprisoned queen and her children cast into the water are saved by a prophesying bird.

plays.title: The Water of Life
plays.description: The humble youngest prince fetches the water of life and, despite betrayal, proves the true saviour.

plays.title: Doctor Knowall
plays.description: A poor peasant becomes the famed Doctor Knowall through a bought sign and lucky chances.

plays.title: The Spirit in the Bottle
plays.description: A poor student's son traps a dangerous spirit in a bottle and turns its gift into his fortune.

plays.title: The Frog Prince
plays.description: The youngest princess promises a frog her love for clear water and frees him after three nights.

plays.title: The Devil's Sooty Brother
plays.description: A discharged soldier serves the Devil seven years, gains gold, and in the end a kingdom.

plays.title: Bearskin
plays.description: A soldier makes a pact with the Devil, holds out seven years, and gains wealth and a faithful bride.

plays.title: The Willow-Wren and the Bear
plays.description: A bear insults the young wrens, loses the animals' war, and must beg for pardon.

plays.title: Sweet Porridge
plays.description: A magic pot feeds the poor, but a forgotten word lets it flood the whole town with porridge.

plays.title: Wise Folks
plays.description: Hans finds two greater fools than his wife and so spares her a beating.

plays.title: The Faithful Animals
plays.description: A man saves a mouse, ape, and bear from torment, and the grateful beasts recover his lost castle.

plays.title: Stories About Snakes
plays.description: Three short scenes show how trust between human and toad breaks apart.

plays.title: The Poor Miller's Boy and the Cat
plays.description: Simple Hans serves an enchanted cat seven years and is made rich for it.

plays.title: The Two Travellers
plays.description: A kind tailor is blinded by a cruel cobbler, but grateful animals raise him to the king's son-in-law.

plays.title: The Crows
plays.description: A blinded soldier left at the gallows overhears three crows, heals the princess, and punishes them.

plays.title: Hans the Hedgehog
plays.description: A half-enchanted son wins his redemption and his kingdom by a promise kept.

plays.title: The Shroud
plays.description: A mother weeps for her dead child until its night-time plea leads her to quiet acceptance.

plays.title: The Miser in the Thorns
plays.description: A mild servant makes a greedy miser dance and turns his false charge at the gallows back on him.

plays.title: The Skilful Huntsman
plays.description: A locksmith's son becomes a never-missing huntsman, slays three giants, and unmasks the false captain.

plays.title: The Flail from Heaven
plays.description: A boastful peasant fetches a flail down from Heaven as proof of his tale.

plays.title: The Two Kings' Children
plays.description: A magic-wielding princess saves the cursed prince and wins him back after his forgetting.

plays.title: The Cunning Little Tailor
plays.description: A young tailor solves the proud princess's riddle and slyly survives the night with the bear.

plays.title: The Bright Sun Brings It to Light
plays.description: A journeyman murders a poor wayfarer, but the sun brings the deed to light.

plays.title: The Blue Light
plays.description: A cast-out soldier finds the blue light and, with its dwarf, brings the king to his knees.

plays.title: The Wilful Child
plays.description: A wilful child finds peace in the grave only when its mother strikes its little arm back down.

plays.title: The Three Army-Surgeons
plays.description: Three boastful army surgeons lose their own limbs through a night-time mix-up.

plays.title: The Seven Swabians
plays.description: Seven Swabians on one spear mistake every scare for death and drown at last at a frog's croak.

plays.title: The Lazy and the Industrious
plays.description: Two journeymen find enchanted ravens; the diligent one is freed by love while the lazy raven dies.

plays.title: The Three Apprentices
plays.description: Three penniless apprentices make a pact with the Devil and barely escape the gallows.

plays.title: The King's Son Who Feared Nothing
plays.description: A fearless prince endures betrayal and torture and frees an enchanted princess.

plays.title: Donkey Cabbages
plays.description: A robbed huntsman turns his cheats into donkeys with magic cabbage and forces penance and peace.

plays.title: The Long Nose
plays.description: Three soldiers lose their magic gifts to a princess and win them back with a nose-stretching charm.

plays.title: The Old Woman in the Wood
plays.description: An orphaned girl trusts in God and frees the enchanted prince with a plain ring.

plays.title: The Three Brothers
plays.description: Three brothers each learn a trade, vie for their father's house, and stay united until death.

plays.title: The Devil and His Grandmother
plays.description: Three soldiers escape the Devil because the third overhears the riddle and answer in the rock house.

plays.title: Ferdinand the Faithful
plays.description: A poor boy rises from outrider to king through loyalty and a talking horse.

plays.title: The Iron Stove
plays.description: A princess frees a prince enchanted into an iron stove and wins him back despite a broken word.

plays.title: The Lazy Spinner
plays.description: A work-shy wife tricks her good-natured husband, ruse after ruse, into giving up spinning for good.

plays.title: The Four Skilful Brothers
plays.description: Four brothers each learn a craft and together rescue the stolen princess.

plays.title: The Lion and the Frog
plays.description: A princess serves a lion in a magic garden and frees him and a tree-frog through obedience and courage.

plays.title: One-Eye, Two-Eyes, and Three-Eyes
plays.description: A despised girl saves herself with a magic goat and a golden apple tree and finds her fortune.

plays.title: The Soldier and the Carpenter
plays.description: A soldier and his timid comrade free a castle by killing three magic beings and a witch.

plays.title: Fair Katrinelje and Pif-Paf-Poltrie
plays.description: A broom-binder gathers each family member's consent in turn to marry fair Katrinelje.

plays.title: The Fox and the Horse
plays.description: With a sly fox's help, a cast-out horse brings a lion to the farmer and is taken back in.

plays.title: The Twelve Dancing Princesses
plays.description: A soldier with a cloak of invisibility uncovers the secret of the princesses' danced-out shoes.

plays.title: The Six Servants
plays.description: A prince wins a proud princess with the help of six servants of supernatural powers.

plays.title: The White Bride and the Black Bride
plays.description: A wicked stepmother foists her black daughter on the king until the white bride is saved as a duck.

plays.title: Iron Hans
plays.description: A prince frees wild Iron Hans, who helps him win a princess while hidden as a gardener's boy.

plays.title: The Wild Man
plays.description: An enchanted wild man helps a banished prince win battles and inherit a kingdom.

plays.title: The Three Black Princesses
plays.description: Three black princesses are nearly freed until the fisher's son pours boiling water on them.

plays.title: Knoist and His Three Sons
plays.description: Three infirm sons set out and meet nothing but senseless impossibilities.

plays.title: The Maid of Brakel
plays.description: A girl prays for a husband, mistakes the mocking sexton for the saint, and scolds the Christ-child.

plays.title: Domestic Servants
plays.description: Two speakers pile up their household in call-and-response and set off together for Walpe.

plays.title: The Lambkin and the Little Fish
plays.description: An envious witch-stepmother turns two loving siblings into animals until a wise woman frees them.

plays.title: Simeli Mountain
plays.description: The poor brother takes only gold, but the greedy one forgets the word and loses his head.

plays.title: Going A-Travelling
plays.description: A simple son goes travelling and is beaten everywhere for repeating learned words in the wrong place.

plays.title: The Famine Children
plays.description: A starving mother threatens to kill her daughters, but they sleep until Judgement Day dawns.

plays.title: The Donkey
plays.description: A prince born a donkey wins a bride with his lute and sheds his hide.

plays.title: The Ungrateful Son
plays.description: A man hides the roast fowl from his old father and forever wears a toad on his face as punishment.

plays.title: The Turnip
plays.description: A poor brother grows a giant turnip, is richly rewarded, and escapes his brother's envy by guile.

plays.title: The Old Man Made Young Again
plays.description: A smith clumsily copies God's rejuvenation miracle and brings only disaster.

plays.title: The Lord's Animals and the Devil's
plays.description: The Devil botches the goats, the Lord punishes them, and in the end the cheat takes its eternal toll.

plays.title: The Beam
plays.description: A girl sees through the sorcerer's illusion but is herself blinded on her wedding day.

plays.title: The Old Beggar-Woman
plays.description: An old beggar woman catches fire at the hearth while a boy looks on without putting it out.

plays.title: The Three Sluggards
plays.description: A dying king leaves his realm to the laziest of his three sons.

plays.title: The Twelve Idle Servants
plays.description: Twelve idle servants lie in the grass at evening, outboasting one another with their own sloth.

plays.title: The Shepherd Boy
plays.description: A shepherd boy answers the king's three impossible questions with shrewd wisdom.

plays.title: The Holy Saint Kummernis
plays.description: A pious maiden prays God for a beard, is crucified, and later saves a fiddler through a miracle.

plays.title: The Star-Money
plays.description: A pious orphan gives away all she owns and is showered with star-coins from Heaven.

plays.title: The Stolen Farthings
plays.description: A dead child returns home until its hidden guilt is atoned and it is laid to rest.

plays.title: Brides on Their Trial
plays.description: A shepherd tests three equally pretty sisters by their cheese and weds the temperate one.

plays.title: Odds and Ends
plays.description: A lazy maid throws away her flax scraps, but the diligent one spins them and wins the bridegroom.

plays.title: The Sparrow and His Four Children
plays.description: An old sparrow instructs his four sons and praises the youngest's trust in God.

plays.title: The Story of Schlauraffen Land
plays.description: A braggart boasts of impossible wonders until a cock closes the tall tale.

plays.title: The Ditmarsch Tale of Wonders
plays.description: A teller strings impossibilities into one tall tale and, at the end, has the window opened.

plays.title: A Riddling Tale
plays.description: Three enchanted flowers look exactly alike, but the dew betrays the one that can be freed.

plays.title: Snow-White and Rose-Red
plays.description: Two kind sisters shelter a bear and free him from the curse of the ungrateful dwarf.

plays.title: The Wise Servant
plays.description: Instead of the lost cow, a servant goes hunting three blackbirds and calls it wise.

plays.title: The Glass Coffin
plays.description: A poor tailor's journeyman frees an enchanted maiden from the glass coffin and wins her hand.

plays.title: Lazy Harry
plays.description: Out of sheer laziness Harry trades everything away until the honey-pot shatters.

plays.title: The Griffin
plays.description: A simple but courteous peasant's son passes every royal test and wins the realm.

plays.title: Strong Hans
plays.description: Bear-strong Hans frees his mother, masters the dwarf, and saves the princess from betrayal.

plays.title: The Peasant in Heaven
plays.description: A poor man and a rich lord die together, but only the rare rich one is welcomed to Heaven with music.

plays.title: Lean Lisa
plays.description: Lean Lisa frets over dreamed-up riches and falls out with lanky Lenz.

plays.title: The Hut in the Forest
plays.description: An enchanted man is freed by the kindness of a daughter who first tends his animals.

plays.title: Sharing Joy and Sorrow
plays.description: A cruel tailor twists the rule to share joy and sorrow, and gets his due reward.

plays.title: The Willow-Wren
plays.description: A tiny bird cons his way to the title of king and is mocked for it forever.

plays.title: The Sole
plays.description: Out of envy at the herring's win, the sole twists its mouth crooked forever.

plays.title: The Bittern and the Hoopoe
plays.description: An old herdsman tells how two intemperate herders lost their flocks and became calling birds.

plays.title: The Owl
plays.description: A whole town fears a harmless owl and burns its own barn to the ground.

plays.title: The Moon
plays.description: Four fellows steal the moon, take it to the grave, and Saint Peter hangs it in the sky.

plays.title: The Misfortune
plays.description: A poor man tries to flee misfortune but is chased by wolves and water and crushed by a wall.

plays.title: The Duration of Life
plays.description: God parcels the animals' rejected years onto man, and so explains his seventy-year life.

plays.title: Death's Messengers
plays.description: Death sends his messengers ahead before he takes the heedless man at last.

plays.title: Master Pfriem
plays.description: A restless faultfinder scolds even Heaven's order and stays incorrigible to the end.

plays.title: The Goose-Girl at the Well
plays.description: A cast-out princess herds geese in ugly skin until an old woman returns her to her rueful parents.

plays.title: Eve's Various Children
plays.description: The Lord blesses Eve's fair and plain children unequally and so orders the ranks as limbs of one body.

plays.title: The Nix of the Millpond
plays.description: A promised child sinks into the pond, but faithful love draws it back by moonlight.

plays.title: The Little Folks' Presents
plays.description: A content tailor keeps his gold; the greedy goldsmith loses all and gains a second hump besides.

plays.title: The Pea Test
plays.description: A princess proves her birth by feeling the peas hidden under the mattresses as she sleeps.

plays.title: The Giant and the Tailor
plays.description: A boastful tailor so frightens a giant that the giant flings him into the air by a trick.

plays.title: The Nail
plays.description: A hurried merchant ignores a missing nail and so loses his horse and his time.

plays.title: The Poor Boy in the Grave
plays.description: An abused orphan seeks death in what he takes for poison and dies in an open grave.

plays.title: The True Sweethearts
plays.description: A faithful maiden loses her beloved to forgetting and wins him back through three dances.

plays.title: The Hare and the Hedgehog
plays.description: A mocked hedgehog outwits the haughty hare with his look-alike wife and wins the wager.

plays.title: The Spindle, the Shuttle, and the Needle
plays.description: A poor, diligent orphan wins the prince as husband through her godmother's three magic gifts.

plays.title: The Peasant and the Devil
plays.description: A cunning peasant outwits the Devil twice over the harvest and wins the treasure.

plays.title: The Crumbs on the Table
plays.description: A peasant tempts his little dogs to nibble and laughs at the beating they get.

plays.title: The Sea-Hare
plays.description: The youngest brother hides from the all-seeing princess with grateful animals' help and wins her.

plays.title: The Robber and His Sons
plays.description: A reformed robber tells the queen two daring tales to save the lives of his three sons.

plays.title: The Master-Thief
plays.description: A lost son returns home a master thief and passes the count's three impossible thieving tests.

plays.title: The Drummer
plays.description: A young drummer frees an enchanted princess from the glass mountain and wins her back after forgetting.

plays.title: The Ear of Corn
plays.description: Ungrateful sacrilege against the blessed grain brings a judgement softened only for the innocent.

plays.title: The Grave-Mound
plays.description: A fearless soldier outwits the Devil with a bottomless boot and saves the miser's soul.

plays.title: Old Rinkrank
plays.description: A captive princess catches old Rinkrank by his beard and escapes down his magic ladder.

plays.title: The Crystal Ball
plays.description: The youngest son frees the enchanted princess with the crystal ball hidden in a fiery egg.

plays.title: Maid Maleen
plays.description: Walled in and impoverished, Maleen wins her prince through constancy and unmasks the false bride.

plays.title: The Boots of Buffalo-Leather
plays.description: A fearless soldier captures twelve robbers and is rewarded by the disguised king.

plays.title: The Golden Key
plays.description: A poor boy finds a golden key, digs up a little box, and turns it, but the lid stays shut.

plays.title: St. Joseph in the Forest
plays.description: Saint Joseph tests three daughters in the forest, but only the most pious is richly rewarded.

plays.title: The Twelve Apostles
plays.description: A poor mother prays for twelve sons, who sleep three hundred years and wake as the apostles.

plays.title: The Rose
plays.description: A strange child gives a poor girl a rose and takes her home when it blooms.

plays.title: Poverty and Humility Lead to Heaven
plays.description: A prince chooses poverty and humility and, through seven years of begging, finds the way to Heaven.

plays.title: God's Food
plays.description: A poor widow is turned away by her rich sister, and her children starve.

plays.title: The Three Green Twigs
plays.description: A pious hermit condemns a sinner, atones with a withered branch, and dies as three green twigs sprout.

plays.title: Our Lady's Little Glass
plays.description: A carter gives the Mother of God wine from a little flower, and his cart is freed.

plays.title: The Aged Mother
plays.description: A lonely widow sees in the church of the dead the spared fate of her sons and dies consoled.

plays.title: The Heavenly Wedding
plays.description: A poor boy walks always straight ahead, takes the church for Heaven, and dies to the heavenly wedding.

plays.title: The Hazel-Branch
plays.description: Mary flees a snake behind a hazel bush and blesses it ever after as a shield against all vermin.
